user_query:
I’m preparing a brief methods appendix for a pilot scan of how official health education websites discuss body image and self-esteem. Please build a small, reproducible command-line tool that reads settings from config/scrape_config.yaml and produces three artifacts under output/: (1) raw HTML snapshots of matched pages, (2) a CSV of per-page term counts, and (3) a JSON file of summary statistics. Requirements:

Inputs and configuration
- Read all settings from config/scrape_config.yaml (do not hardcode domains, keywords, or limits). The config specifies: allowed_domains, topics (keywords used to pre-filter URLs), positive_terms, negative_terms, max_pages_per_domain, output_dir, user_agent, and min_char_len.

Discovery and download
- For each domain in allowed_domains, fetch its robots.txt and discover any Sitemap entries. Download and parse the XML sitemap(s) to obtain candidate URLs. If no sitemaps are found for a domain, record this in the log and continue with other domains.
- Filter candidate URLs by the topics keywords from the config using a case-insensitive match in the URL string. From the filtered set, process up to max_pages_per_domain per domain.
- Download each selected page using the configured user_agent. Save the raw HTML to output/pages/<domain>/<a filesystem-safe slug>.html. Create directories as needed.

Text extraction and counting
- Extract visible page text (strip scripts/styles and non-visible content). Skip pages whose extracted text length is less than min_char_len.
- Compute per-page statistics:
  - word_count (number of whitespace-delimited tokens in the visible text)
  - case-insensitive whole-word counts for every term in positive_terms and negative_terms. Use a simple tokenization where non-letter characters split words; count matches on exact tokens (e.g., "self-compassion" should match the token "self" and "compassion" only if that is how your tokenizer yields tokens; hyphens and punctuation act as separators).
  - positive_total (sum of positive term counts) and negative_total (sum of negative term counts)
  - ratio_pos_to_neg = positive_total / max(1, negative_total), rounded to 3 decimals

CSV output
- Write output/articles.csv with one row per downloaded-and-parsed page and these columns exactly:
  - url, domain, title, saved_html_path (relative path under output/pages), word_count, positive_total, negative_total, ratio_pos_to_neg
  - plus one integer column for each configured term in positive_terms and negative_terms named term_<normalized>, where <normalized> is the lowercased term with spaces replaced by underscores and non-alphanumeric characters removed (e.g., "self-compassion" -> "selfcompassion"). The header must include all these term columns even if some counts are zero.
- If no pages are matched or all are skipped by min_char_len, still produce articles.csv with the correct header and zero rows.

Summary JSON
- Write output/stats.json with:
  - run_timestamp (ISO 8601),
  - config_sha256 (SHA-256 of the exact bytes of config/scrape_config.yaml),
  - domains_used (from config), topics_used, positive_terms_used, negative_terms_used, max_pages_per_domain_used,
  - pages_downloaded_total,
  - pages_downloaded_by_domain (object mapping domain -> count),
  - average_word_count_by_domain (object mapping domain -> float, 0.0 if no pages),
  - term_totals (object mapping each normalized term -> total occurrences across all pages),
  - positive_total and negative_total across all pages.

Logging
- Write a plain-text log at output/run.log summarizing: domains processed, number of sitemaps discovered per domain, number of candidate URLs, number of URLs filtered by topics, number downloaded, and number skipped by min_char_len.

General constraints
- Make the Internet step essential by actually discovering sitemaps from robots.txt and downloading live pages from the specified official domains (do not use browser automation or paid APIs). Respect robots.txt and stay within the configured domains. Keep the script deterministic given the config and the current state of the sites.
- The tool must rely on the provided configuration file; do not hardcode domains or term lists. Echo the config values and their SHA-256 into stats.json for cross-checking.

Deliverables to create under the workspace
- The code for the tool (you may choose the language), clearly reading config/scrape_config.yaml.
- output/pages/… with raw HTML snapshots.
- output/articles.csv meeting the column requirements above.
- output/stats.json with the fields above.
- output/run.log summarizing the run.

Input path to use: config/scrape_config.yaml
Output base directory: output/
If anything is ambiguous, prefer clarity and robustness. Please include minimal instructions in a top-level README explaining how to run the tool locally. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
